item.title: Russian Attack on Ukrainian Cities Kills Four People, Zelenskiy Says
item.description: Russia carried out one of the largest attacks on Ukraine overnight, killing four people and leaving over 30 wounded across the country, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Telegram.

item.title: Merz’s Big Year of Economic Renewal in Germany Is Going Awry
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s bid for a meaningful rebound in 2026 is getting harder to deliver by the week, despite the huge stimulus flowing into Europe’s biggest economy.

item.title: Graticule’s Levinson ‘Open’ to Iran Resolution Ahead of Rally
item.description: Investor Adam Levinson said he remains bullish despite recent market volatility, and is buying the dip ahead of an expected rally on a resolution in the Iran conflict.

item.title: PBOC Warns of Finance ‘Involution,’ Reaffirms Risk Control Focus
item.description: China’s central bank and foreign‑exchange regulator reiterated the need to curb excessive — or “involution‑style” — competition within the country’s finance industry, framing it as a governance and discipline concern.

item.title: US Grants Maduro Access to Funds for Defense in Criminal Case
item.description: US officials reversed themselves and will now allow Nicolás Maduro and his wife to pay lawyers with Venezuelan funds as they defend themselves in the drug-trafficking case against them in New York.

item.title: Xi Calls Laos Key Priority in China’s Neighborhood Diplomacy
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping said that Beijing has always viewed its relations with Laos from a strategic and long-term perspective, describing the country as a key priority in China’s diplomacy with neighboring nations.

item.title: China Says US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Chip Supply Chains
item.description: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, which Beijing warned could disrupt global supply chains if passed.

item.title: Malaysia Picks Abdul Halim Bin Aman as Its New Anti-Graft Chief
item.description: Malaysia named Abdul Halim Bin Aman as the nation’s next anti-graft chief, taking over from current head Azam Baki when his term ends next month.

item.title: Denso to Withdraw Proposal to Take Over Rohm, Nikkei Says
item.description: Denso Corp. is set to withdraw its proposal to take over semiconductor maker Rohm Co., as the two sides were unable to see eye to eye on an offer, Nikkei reported.

item.title: Oracle Data Center $16 Billion Financing Gets Over The Line
item.description: A $16 billion financing for a giant Oracle Corp. data center in Michigan has wrapped after months of stop-and-start negotiations with investors.

item.title: AI Chip Surge Elevates Taiwan, Korea in Global Equity Rankings
item.description: The artificial intelligence boom has triggered a seismic reshuffling of global equity markets, with Taiwan and South Korea muscling past European nations one by one.

item.title: DOJ’s Partial Reversal on Powell Probe Keeps Fed Drama Alive
item.description: A decision by US District Attorney Jeanine Pirro to drop a criminal probe into the Federal Reserve potentially clears a path for President Donald Trump’s nominee to take over the central bank. What it won’t do is secure the current Fed chair’s departure.

item.title: Mid-Market Investment Bank Lincoln International Files for IPO
item.description: Lincoln International Inc., a mid-market investment bank that advises business owners and private equity funds, filed for a US initial public offering, disclosing growing net income.

item.title: US Life Insurers Have Shifted More General Account Risk Offshore
item.description: US life insurers have shifted more of their general account risk to entities abroad than in the US as of year-end, the first time that offshore reinsurance hubs have overtaken domestic ones for that business.

item.title: Why Apple Picked Their Product Guy as the Next CEO
item.description: At Apple, 15 years of Tim Cook is coming to an end. What will 15 years under John Ternus look like?

item.title: Belgium Cut by S&P in Second Judgment on Worst Euro-Zone Deficit
item.description: Belgium was cut by S&P Global Ratings, the second downgrade by a credit assessor in a week on the country running the euro area’s biggest budget deficits.

item.title: Wall Street Conviction Frays on Trades Riding Out AI, War Shock
item.description: One by one, high-conviction trades on Wall Street are falling out of favor.

item.title: Nvidia Breakout Sends Chip Giant to First Record Since October
item.description: Nvidia Corp. shares rose to their first record in months on Friday, the latest example of improving momentum at the leader in artificial intelligence chips.

item.title: US Sanctions China Refinery, Iran Shadow Fleet Ahead of Talks
item.description: The US sanctioned a Chinese oil refinery as well as dozens of shipping firms and vessels that transport Iranian oil, as the Trump administration ramps up economic pressure on Tehran ahead of diplomatic talks.

item.title: Copper and Silver Miner Lumina’s Toronto IPO Raises $297 Million
item.description: Lumina Metals Corp. and one of its shareholders raised C$406.2 million ($297 million) in an upsized Canadian initial public offering.

item.title: Goldman Is Avoiding Charity Cases In ETF Market-Making Return
item.description: The Wall Street giant is stepping back in after years on the sidelines—but only for funds it thinks can “reach escape velocity,” says Ashok Varadhan.

item.title: Maine Governor Mills Vetoes Statewide Data Center Moratorium
item.description: Maine Governor Janet Mills vetoed what would have been the first statewide freeze on large data center development, saying it would hurt a part of Maine in need of an economic boost.

item.title: DeepSeek’s Long-Awaited New Model Fails to Narrow US Lead in AI
item.description: When China’s DeepSeek released a competitive new artificial intelligence model called R1 last January purportedly built for less than many rivals, some feared the achievement posed a threat to America’s lead in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Stanford Professor Targets $1 Billion Valuation For AI-for-Physiology Startup
item.description: Stanford University professor James Zou is raising money at a roughly $1 billion valuation to build artificial intelligence models to improve research about the human body, according to sources familiar with the efforts.

item.title: Brazil Setting Higher Ethanol Blend to Stem Fuel Impact From War
item.description: Brazil is taking action to increase the use of ethanol in gasoline in an effort to tame the impacts of rising fuel costs as the war in the Middle East drags on.

item.title: Military-Grade Jet Fuel Cargoes From US Show Scale of War Impact
item.description: An unusual series of cargoes of military-grade fuel that is due to sail from the US across the Pacific Ocean illustrates how far the war in Iran has disrupted the global oil supply chain.

item.title: Sonrava Health Hires Bank To Explore Asset Sales Following Lender Talks
item.description: Sonrava Health, an operator of dental clinics across the country, has hired investment bank Lincoln International LLC to explore options including asset sales, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ricky Sandler Shuttering Eminence Capital After 27-Year Run
item.description: Hedge fund veteran Ricky Sandler is shuttering Eminence Capital and returning cash to investors after a 27-year run.

item.title: Brazil Moves to Ban Prediction Markets on Elections, Sports
item.description: Brazil moved to ban prediction markets tied to elections and sporting events, tightening rules on a booming practice that’s drawing scrutiny across the world.

item.title: Energy Merchant Six One Overtakes Trafigura in US Gas Trading
item.description: Six One Commodities LLC has more than tripled its physical US natural gas trading activity, catapulting the firm above heavyweight commodity houses Trafigura Trading, Freepoint Commodities and Hartree Partners.
